content:
from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 3, 2025 11:48PM
Jennifer Thomas
After reading the story ,“A Worn Path,” by Eudora Welty’s, it can be seen that symbolism plays a significant role in demonstrating the themes of perseverance and sacrifice through the character Phoenix Jackson. This can be seen through Phoenix’s journey in which she went through to obtain medicine for her grandson. Her journey shows a correlation to an individual’s dedication and the sacrifices made for their loved ones. The author uses Phoenix’s name and the journey she endured as symbols to generate these two themes

First and foremost, a Phoenix symbolizes strength since it is a mythical creature capable of going through multiple challenges and hardships it may come across in its life. This relates to the main character by, in Phoenix’s journey, she experiences so many physical and emotional difficulties. She is an old, fragile women who is dealing with lots of problems and yet that doesn’t stop her from going through a strenuous journey for the health of her grandson. According to the story, Eudora demonstrates this by mentioning that "She was very old and small, and she walked slowly, moving a little from side to side in her steps, with balanced heaviness. She carried a thin, small cane made from an umbrella".
